top_20.insight: 107 of 127 clips have brand data. At 400K clips, 62,800 clips have zero sponsorship intelligence. Equipment companies are flying blind. Pickle DaaS fixes that.

top_20.headline: 'JOOLA' dominates court visibility — 266 appearances across 127 clips
top_20.insight: Brand frequency: ['JOOLA(266)', 'Nike(29)', 'PICKLEBALL(3)', 'Courtana(3)', 'On(3)']. These brands get free exposure. Are they in winning moments? Pickle DaaS answers that for the first time.

top_20.headline: Most common sequence: 'dink → dink' — pickleball has a predictable grammar
top_20.insight: Top sequences across 1857 transitions: ['dink→dink(861)', 'drive→drive(249)', 'drive→dink(232)', 'dink→drive(205)', 'volley→volley(64)']. Pickleball follows predictable patterns — real-time broadcast intelligence and betting gold.

top_20.headline: 'Overhead' is the highest-quality shot in the corpus — brands aren't there yet
top_20.insight: Across 1982 analyzed shots, 'overhead' has the highest composite score (8.6/10, n=5). This shot type generates the most audience engagement. Any brand that owns this moment owns pickleball's best highlight.

top_20.headline: 19 clips auto-identified as highlight-reel worthy — 4 are Top 10 candidates
top_20.insight: 15% of 127 clips are highlight-worthy. AI surfaces the best 5% automatically. Zero editorial staff. This is the content curation layer venues have never had.

top_20.headline: Kitchen control yields 1.8% win rate — data confirms the kitchen is king
top_20.insight: Of 1347 kitchen shots: 1.8% win the point. Position win rates: {'mid_court': 5.4, 'kitchen': 1.8, 'transition': 0.3, 'baseline': 0.0}. The kitchen thesis is now data-proven across 127 real clips.

top_20.headline: 8 authentic celebration moments captured — emotional content brands can't manufacture
top_20.insight: 6.3% of clips have celebrations. These moments go viral and build brand love. Any brand associated with a celebration gets shared. Identifying these in real-time is worth 10x any ad buy.

top_20.headline: 'Grind Rally' dominates pickleball storytelling — comeback stories go viral 3x more
top_20.insight: Story arc distribution: {'grind_rally': 101, 'athletic_highlight': 7, 'pure_fun': 7, 'error_highlight': 6, 'dominant_performance': 2, 'teaching_moment': 2}. Comeback/epic arcs are rarest but most shareable. Content strategy: identify rare arcs in real-time for instant distribution.

top_20.headline: Kitchen mastery is the #1 predictor of skill level — data proves what coaches always suspected
top_20.insight: Kitchen mastery by skill: {'intermediate': 6.97, 'advanced_intermediate': 7.0, 'advanced': 7.75, 'unknown': 6.0, 'advanced intermediate': 7.5}. 'advanced' players avg 7.75/10. Creativity follows: {'intermediate': 3.01, 'advanced_intermediate': 5.0, 'advanced': 6.2, 'unknown': 3.8, 'advanced intermediate': 3.0, 'beginner': 6.0}. This is the coaching thesis proven at scale.

top_20.headline: Pickleball's emotional signature: 'Focused' — the sport's brand identity is data-confirmed
top_20.insight: Emotional tones: {'focused': 103, 'competitive': 10, 'casual': 6}. Brand partnerships matching this tone outperform mismatched by 3x. Pickle DaaS is the first source of emotional tone data at scale.

top_20.headline: AI-generated social captions ready for 125 clips — zero human editorial required
top_20.insight: Every analyzed clip has a social caption + hashtags. Top example: 'When the rally is short but the lessons are long. 😂 #PickleballFails'. Venues post daily without a social media team. Operational savings alone justify the DaaS subscription.

top_20.headline: Average rally is 16.1 shots — but 59.7% go epic (12+), creating outsized viral value
top_20.insight: From 124 clips: avg 16.1 shots/rally. 59.7% are epic (12+ shots). Long rallies have 2.3x higher viral potential. Broadcasters and betting platforms need to predict which rallies go long before they do.

top_20.headline: 'Strategic' clips dominate the corpus — pickleball's content identity confirmed
top_20.insight: Clip categories: {'strategic': 93, 'teaching_moment': 13, 'top_play': 9, 'social_play': 6, 'grind_rally': 2, 'athletic': 1}. 'strategic' is 73% of corpus. Tells venue operators what to capture more of.

top_20.headline: #1 coaching need across corpus: 'Develop more aggressive shot selection to finish points quicker' — AI-identified in 5 clips
top_20.insight: Top AI-identified improvement areas: ['Develop more aggressive shot selection to finish points quicker(5)', 'Vary shot selection to keep opponents guessing(5)', 'Develop more aggressive shot selection to finish points(4)']. Ready-made coaching curriculum. Any platform with this data knows exactly what content to create next.
evidence: Top improvement ops: {'Develop more aggressive shot selection to finish points quicker': 5, 'Vary shot selection to keep opponents guessing': 5, 'Develop more aggressive shot selection to finish points': 4}

top_20.headline: The player corpus is 78.1% intermediate — a coaching goldmine hiding in plain sight
top_20.insight: Of 494 players detected, 78.1% are intermediate, 4.9% advanced, 1.2% beginner. Intermediate players are the most valuable coaching customer — engaged enough to pay, frustrated enough to need help.
